message:
br-bd-oqphq add largest_contiguous_free_run for fragmentation-aware statvfs

Adds the API piece bd-oqphq calls out in its acceptance:
  pub fn OpenFs::largest_contiguous_free_run(&self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<u64>

Backed by a new pure helper in ffs-alloc:
  pub fn bitmap_largest_free_run(bitmap: &[u8], count: u32) -> u32

ext4 path scans every group's block bitmap and returns the per-group
maximum (ext4 bitmaps live one-per-group, so on-disk free runs never
straddle a group boundary — per-group max is the natural unit).

btrfs path returns total_free_bytes / sectorsize as a conservative upper
bound (btrfs has no per-group bitmap; precise tracking would require
walking the extent tree's free-space map).

Fast bitmap scan: 0x00 bytes contribute 8 each via a fast skip; 0xFF
breaks the run; partial bytes fall back to per-bit inspection. Trailing
remainder bits past `count % 8` are honoured. Missing bytes (truncated
bitmap) break the in-flight run, mirroring `bitmap_count_free`'s
conservative semantics.

Tests:
- 10 ffs-alloc unit tests cover empty/all-zero/all-one bitmaps, single
  middle run, multiple runs picking max, partial-byte boundaries,
  byte-spanning runs, count-smaller-than-byte-remainder, full-zero-byte
  extension, and matches-count_free invariant.
- 2 ffs-core integration tests against build_ext4_image_with_inode:
  longest_run <= total_free invariant and post-bitmap-mutation drop.

Verification:
- rch cargo test -p ffs-alloc -p ffs-core --lib: 157 + 644 passing.
- rch cargo clippy -p ffs-alloc -p ffs-core --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings: clean.
- rustfmt --check on touched files: clean.

The remaining bd-oqphq acceptance (statvfs accuracy under writeback,
xfstests generic/{006,027,035}) requires the FUSE mount path and
xfstests harness; the building-block API + invariant tests this commit
ships are the prerequisite they consume.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-oq04","title":"Add btrfs COW update allocator-failure mutation coverage","description":"Apply testing-fuzzing fallback to the in-memory btrfs COW tree update mutation path: add adversarial allocator-failure coverage for leaf-update and parent-rewrite failures, prove failed updates do not change visible tree contents or publish deferred frees, and update conformance/COVERAGE.md with the new mutation coverage evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T11:53:55.126378121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T12:03:33.779536539Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T12:03:33.776146653Z","close_reason":"Completed btrfs COW update allocator-failure mutation coverage; focused tests, workspace check, workspace clippy, fmt, diff check, UBS critical scan, and routed disk-hygiene clean completed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","mutation","testing"]}
-{"id":"bd-oqphq","title":"[FUSE] statvfs filesystem-level metadata + free-space fragmentation","description":"Implement statvfs(3) / FUSE_STATFS so callers see accurate filesystem-level numbers — total/free/avail blocks + inodes — and a fragmentation-aware available count.

Context:
- POSIX statvfs requires: f_bsize, f_frsize, f_blocks, f_bfree, f_bavail, f_files, f_ffree, f_favail, f_fsid, f_flag, f_namemax. The kernel calls FUSE_STATFS (opcode 17), the FUSE driver translates the fuse_kstatfs reply into the kernel statfs.
- ext4 sources these from the superblock + group-descriptor cached counts (Ext4Superblock::s_free_blocks_count_lo + ext4 group desc bg_free_blocks_count) — these can drift from the actual bitmap state if a previous mount crashed before persisting.
- For accuracy under writeback the report must subtract pending uncommitted writes (MVCC version chains hold dirty blocks that look free in the bitmap but are actually in use).
- Free-space fragmentation: f_bavail traditionally = f_bfree minus reserved blocks. But for allocators that need contiguous regions (large extent writes), exposing the largest-contiguous-free-run via a side channel (e.g., FIEMAP on a synthetic free-space inode) helps callers like fallocate(FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE) plan ahead.

ffs-fuse already implements FsOps::statfs but the result is mostly hard-coded for tests; need to consult the actual ffs-alloc::GroupStats sums and subtract MVCC-pending writes.

Acceptance:
- Test: statvfs(mount_point).f_bavail decreases by N after writing N blocks.
- Test: after fallocate(KEEP_SIZE, big), f_bavail decreases by big-blocks (preallocated unwritten extents still consume space).
- A new ffs-core API: largest_contiguous_free_run() returns the size of the longest run of zero bits in the block bitmap, used by tests + future free-space-FIEMAP work.
- xfstests generic/{006, 027, 035} (statvfs accuracy) pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:29:37.163677090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:29:37.163677090Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-oqphq","title":"[FUSE] statvfs filesystem-level metadata + free-space fragmentation","description":"Implement statvfs(3) / FUSE_STATFS so callers see accurate filesystem-level numbers — total/free/avail blocks + inodes — and a fragmentation-aware available count.

Context:
- POSIX statvfs requires: f_bsize, f_frsize, f_blocks, f_bfree, f_bavail, f_files, f_ffree, f_favail, f_fsid, f_flag, f_namemax. The kernel calls FUSE_STATFS (opcode 17), the FUSE driver translates the fuse_kstatfs reply into the kernel statfs.
- ext4 sources these from the superblock + group-descriptor cached counts (Ext4Superblock::s_free_blocks_count_lo + ext4 group desc bg_free_blocks_count) — these can drift from the actual bitmap state if a previous mount crashed before persisting.
- For accuracy under writeback the report must subtract pending uncommitted writes (MVCC version chains hold dirty blocks that look free in the bitmap but are actually in use).
- Free-space fragmentation: f_bavail traditionally = f_bfree minus reserved blocks. But for allocators that need contiguous regions (large extent writes), exposing the largest-contiguous-free-run via a side channel (e.g., FIEMAP on a synthetic free-space inode) helps callers like fallocate(FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE) plan ahead.

ffs-fuse already implements FsOps::statfs but the result is mostly hard-coded for tests; need to consult the actual ffs-alloc::GroupStats sums and subtract MVCC-pending writes.

Acceptance:
- Test: statvfs(mount_point).f_bavail decreases by N after writing N blocks.
- Test: after fallocate(KEEP_SIZE, big), f_bavail decreases by big-blocks (preallocated unwritten extents still consume space).
- A new ffs-core API: largest_contiguous_free_run() returns the size of the longest run of zero bits in the block bitmap, used by tests + future free-space-FIEMAP work.
- xfstests generic/{006, 027, 035} (statvfs accuracy) pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:29:37.163677090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:02:09.942417878Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T10:02:09.940374614Z","close_reason":"Added bitmap_largest_free_run helper in ffs-alloc + OpenFs::largest_contiguous_free_run for ext4 (per-group bitmap scan max) and btrfs (extent_alloc free-byte upper bound). 10 ffs-alloc unit tests + 2 ffs-core integration tests; rch lib tests 157+644 passing, clippy clean, fmt clean on touched lines.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-orbjt","title":"[HIGH] ffs-repair decode_group succeeds when all blocks corrupt","description":"codec::tests::fault_injection_progressive_corruption fails: decode_group returns Ok when all source blocks are marked corrupt. Expected behavior is Err (cannot recover without any valid source blocks). Test at crates/ffs-repair/src/codec.rs:888. Potential data integrity issue - codec claims recovery success when mathematically impossible.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:58:24.258837624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:23:35Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:23:35Z","close_reason":"decode_group now rejects zero-intact-source recovery attempts before invoking the decoder, the progressive corruption regression passes remotely, and the repair codec fuzz oracle was aligned with the same contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ortqa","title":"add mounted-path btrfs getxattr/listxattr size-probe and ERANGE coverage","description":"Survey finding: ffs-fuse exposes the same Linux xattr size=0 probe and undersized-buffer ERANGE behavior on btrfs mounts, but fuse_e2e only proves successful payload reads. Scope: add btrfs mounted-path probes for getxattr and listxattr with zero-sized length discovery plus undersized-buffer ERANGE on real FUSE paths; assert exact reported lengths, exact errno, and no xattr/file mutation. Acceptance: btrfs E2E proves size-probe and too-small-buffer behavior for both getxattr and listxattr.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:02:38.242327184Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:03:24.116056860Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:03:24.114163685Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-osoe","title":"Add fuzz coverage for btrfs send-stream parsing","description":"Add a dedicated cargo-fuzz target for ffs-btrfs send/receive stream parsing, seed it with minimal valid and adversarial corpora, and verify the fuzz workspace remains structurally sound under the dynamic target inventory.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-16T22:21:48.056839367Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T22:30:30.124254306Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T22:30:30.121832801Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzzing","testing"]}
